member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureAddressModeContent
member.summary: Defines modes for addressing texels using texture coordinates that are outside of the range of 0.0 to 1.0.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureAddressModeContent.Wrap
member.summary: Texels outside range will form the tile at every integer junction.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureAddressModeContent.Clamp
member.summary: Texels outside range will be set to color of 0.0 or 1.0 texel.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureAddressModeContent.Mirror
member.summary: Same as but tiles will also flipped at every integer junction.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureAddressModeContent.Border
member.summary: Texels outside range will be set to the border color.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureFilterContent
member.summary: Defines filtering types for texture sampler.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureFilterContent.Linear
member.summary: Use linear filtering.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureFilterContent.Point
member.summary: Use point filtering.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureFilterContent.Anisotropic
member.summary: Use anisotropic filtering.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureFilterContent.LinearMipPoint
member.summary: Use linear filtering to shrink or expand, and point filtering between mipmap levels (mip).

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureFilterContent.PointMipLinear
member.summary: Use point filtering to shrink (minify) or expand (magnify), and linear filtering between mipmap levels.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureFilterContent.MinLinearMagPointMipLinear
member.summary: Use linear filtering to shrink, point filtering to expand, and linear filtering between mipmap levels.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureFilterContent.MinLinearMagPointMipPoint
member.summary: Use linear filtering to shrink, point filtering to expand, and point filtering between mipmap levels.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureFilterContent.MinPointMagLinearMipLinear
member.summary: Use point filtering to shrink, linear filtering to expand, and linear filtering between mipmap levels.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureFilterContent.MinPointMagLinearMipPoint
member.summary: Use point filtering to shrink, linear filtering to expand, and point filtering between mipmap levels.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.TextureFilterModeContent
member.summary: Filtering modes for texture samplers.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.CompareFunctionContent

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.CompareFunctionContent.Always
member.summary: Always passes the test.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.CompareFunctionContent.Never
member.summary: Never passes the test.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.CompareFunctionContent.Less
member.summary: Passes the test when the new pixel value is less than current pixel value.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.CompareFunctionContent.LessEqual
member.summary: Passes the test when the new pixel value is less than or equal to current pixel value.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.CompareFunctionContent.Equal
member.summary: Passes the test when the new pixel value is equal to current pixel value.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.CompareFunctionContent.GreaterEqual
member.summary: Passes the test when the new pixel value is greater than or equal to current pixel value.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.CompareFunctionContent.Greater
member.summary: Passes the test when the new pixel value is greater than current pixel value.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.EffectCompiler.CompareFunctionContent.NotEqual
member.summary: Passes the test when the new pixel value does not equal to current pixel value.

member.summary: A bin that can pack rectangles using the MaxRects algorithm with several heuristics.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.BinWidth
member.summary: Width of the bin.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.BinHeight
member.summary: Height of the bin.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.AllowRotations
member.summary: Indicates if rectangle can be rotated.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.PaddingWidth
member.summary: Padding to add to both sides in the horizontal dimension. Default is 0.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.PaddingHeight
member.summary: Padding to add to both sides in the vertical dimension. Default is 0.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.IncludePadding
member.summary: Determines if padding is included in returned rectangles. Default is false.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.UsedWidth
member.summary: The actually used width of the bin.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.UsedHeight
member.summary: The actually used height of the bin.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.GrowLimit
member.summary: The number of times to grow the bin for a single rectangle when it doesn't fit. Defaults to 3.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.Grow
member.summary: Rule to choose in which dimension to grow.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.GrowIncrement
member.summary: Determines how the bin is grown. A positive value means a fixed size increase, a negative value means a factor increase. E.g. -2 will grow the bin by a factor of 2 when it needs to grow.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.GrowRule,System.Single,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Create a instance.
member.param: Initial available width. Defaults to 64.
Initial available height. Defaults to 64.
Whether to allow growing the available space when a rectangle doesn't fit.
Determines how the bin is grown. A positive value means a fixed size increase, a negative value means a factor increase. E.g. -2 will grow the bin by a factor of 2 when it needs to grow.
Whether to allow rotating the rectangles.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsBin.GetOccupancy(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Computes the ratio of used surface area.
member.param: If true this uses and instead of and for available area.

members.member: Returns 0 if the two intervals i1 and i2 are disjoint, or the length of their overlap otherwise.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsHeuristic
member.summary: Heuristic method to use to choose the rectangles positions.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsHeuristic.Bssf
member.summary: Best Short Side Fit: Positions the rectangle against the short side of a free rectangle into which it fits the best.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsHeuristic.Blsf
member.summary: Best Long Side Fit: Positions the rectangle against the long side of a free rectangle into which it fits the best.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsHeuristic.Baf
member.summary: Best Area Fit: Positions the rectangle into the smallest free rect into which it fits.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsHeuristic.Bl
member.summary: Bottom Left (Tetris method): Each rectangle is placed to a position (possibly rotating it) where its top side lies as low as possible.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaxRectsHeuristic.RectContactPointRule
member.summary: Contact Point: Choosest the placement where the rectangle touches other rects as much as possible.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.LocalizedFontDescription
member.summary: Normally, when you add a .spritefont file to your project, this data is deserialized into a FontDescription object, which is then built into a SpriteFontContent by the FontDescriptionProcessor. But to localize the font, we want to add some additional data, so our custom processor can know what .resx files it needs to scan. We do this by defining our own custom font description class, deriving from the built in FontDescription type, and adding a new property to store the resource filenames.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.LocalizedFontDescription.#ctor
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.LocalizedFontDescription.ResourceFiles
member.summary: Add a new property to our font description, which will allow us to include a ResourceFiles element in the .spritefont XML. We use the ContentSerializer attribute to mark this as optional, so existing .spritefont files that do not include this ResourceFiles element can be imported as well.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaterialContent.SetTexture(System.String,Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.ExternalReference{Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.TextureContent})
member.summary: Sets a value in the contained TextureReferenceDictionary object. If null is passed, the value is removed.
member.param: Name of the key being modified.
Value being set.
member.remarks: The key value differs depending on the type of attached dictionary. If attached to a BasicMaterialContent dictionary (which becomes a BasicEffect object at run time), the value for the Texture key is used as the texture for the BasicEffect runtime object. Other keys are ignored. If attached to a EffectMaterialContent dictionary, key names are the texture names used by the effect. These names are dependent upon the author of the effect object.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.VertexContent
member.summary: Provides methods and properties for maintaining the vertex data of a GeometryContent.
member.remarks: This class combines a collection of arbitrarily named data channels with a list of position indices that reference the Positions collection of the parent MeshContent.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.VertexContent.Channels
member.summary: Gets the list of named vertex data channels in the VertexContent.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.VertexContent.PositionIndices
member.summary: Gets the list of position indices.
member.value: Position of the position index being retrieved.
member.remarks: This list adds a level of indirection between the actual triangle indices and the Positions member of the parent. This indirection preserves the topological vertex identity in cases where a single vertex position is used by triangles that straddle a discontinuity in some other data channel. For example, the following code gets the position of the first vertex of the first triangle in a GeometryContent object: parent.Positions[Vertices.PositionIndices[Indices[0]]]

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.VertexContent.Positions
member.summary: Gets position data from the parent mesh object.
member.value: Collection of vertex positions for the mesh.
member.remarks: The collection returned from this call provides a virtualized view of the vertex positions for this batch. The collection uses the contents of the PositionIndices property to index into the parent Positions. This collection is read-only. If you need to modify any contained values, edit the PositionIndices or Positions members directly.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.VertexContent.VertexCount
member.summary: Number of vertices for the content.

member.param: Index into the MeshContent.Positions member of the parent.
member.returns: Index of the new entry. This can be added to the Indices member of the parent.

member.summary: Appends multiple vertex indices to the end of the PositionIndices collection. Other vertex channels will automatically be extended and the new indices populated with default values.

member.exception: One or more of the vertex channel types are invalid or an unrecognized name was passed to VertexElementUsage.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.LocalizedFontProcessor
member.summary: Custom processor extends the SpriteFont build process to scan over the resource strings used by the game, automatically adding whatever characters it finds in them to the font. This makes sure the game will always have all the characters it needs, no matter what languages it is localized into, while still producing an efficient font that does not waste space on unnecessary characters. This is especially useful for languages such as Japanese and Korean, which have potentially thousands of different characters, although games typically only use a small fraction of these. Building only the characters we need is far more efficient than if we tried to include the entire CJK character region.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.LocalizedFontProcessor.Process(Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.LocalizedFontDescription,Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.ContentProcessorContext)
member.summary: Converts a font description into SpriteFont format.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.MaterialProcessor
member.summary: Provides methods and properties for maintaining a collection of named texture references.
member.remarks: In addition to texture references, opaque data values are stored in the OpaqueData property of the base class.

member.summary: Gets or sets the color value to replace with transparent black.
member.value: Color value of the material to replace with transparent black.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.MaterialProcessor.ColorKeyEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether color keying of a texture is enabled.
member.value: true if color keying is enabled; false otherwise.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.MaterialProcessor.DefaultEffect
member.summary: The default effect type for this instance of MaterialProcessor.
member.remarks: When MaterialProcessor is instantiated, DefaultEffect is set to default to BasicEffect Class.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.MaterialProcessor.GenerateMipmaps
member.summary: Specifies if a full chain of mipmaps are generated from the source material. Existing mipmaps of the material are not replaced.
member.value: true if mipmap generation is enabled; false otherwise.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.MaterialProcessor.PremultiplyTextureAlpha
member.summary: Specifies whether alpha premultiply of textures is enabled.
member.value: true if alpha premultiply is enabled; false otherwise.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.MaterialProcessor.ResizeTexturesToPowerOfTwo
member.summary: Specifies whether resizing of a material is enabled. Typically used to maximize compatability with a graphics card because many graphics cards do not support a material size that is not a power of two. If ResizeTexturesToPowerOfTwo is enabled, the material is resized to the next largest power of two.
member.value: true if resizing is enabled; false otherwise.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.MaterialProcessor.TextureFormat
member.summary: Specifies the texture format of output materials. Materials can either be left unchanged from the source asset, converted to a corresponding Color, or compressed using the appropriate DxtCompressed format.
member.value: The texture format of the output.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.MaterialProcessor.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the MaterialProcessor class.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.MaterialProcessor.BuildEffect(Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.ExternalReference{Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.EffectContent},Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.ContentProcessorContext)
member.summary: Builds effect content.
member.param: An external reference to the effect content.
Context for the specified processor.
member.remarks: If the input to process is of type EffectMaterialContent, this function will be called to request that the EffectContent be built. The EffectProcessor is used to process the EffectContent. Subclasses of MaterialProcessor can override this function to modify the parameters used to build EffectContent. For example, a different version of this function could request a different processor for the EffectContent.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.MaterialProcessor.BuildTexture(System.String,Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.ExternalReference{Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.TextureContent},Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.ContentProcessorContext)
member.summary: Builds texture content.
member.param: The name of the texture. This should correspond to the key used to store the texture in Textures.
The asset to build. This should be a member of Textures.
Context for the specified processor.
member.remarks: textureName can be used to determine which processor to use. For example, if a texture is being used as a normal map, the user may not want to use the ModelTextureProcessor on it, which compresses textures.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.MaterialProcessor.Process(Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Graphics.MaterialContent,Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.ContentProcessorContext)
member.summary: Builds the texture and effect content for the material.
member.param: The material content to build.
Context for the specified processor.
member.remarks: If the MaterialContent is of type EffectMaterialContent, a build is requested for Effect, and validation will be performed on the OpaqueData to ensure that all parameters are valid input to SetValue or SetValueTranspose. If the MaterialContent is a BasicMaterialContent, no validation will be performed on OpaqueData. Process requests builds for all textures in Textures, unless the MaterialContent is of type BasicMaterialContent, in which case a build will only be requested for DiffuseColor. The textures in Textures will be ignored.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.TextureProcessorOutputFormat
member.summary: Specifies the target output (of type SurfaceFormat) of the texture processor. Used by TextureProcessor.TextureFormat.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.TextureProcessorOutputFormat.Color
member.summary: The SurfaceFormat value, of the input TextureContent object, is converted to Color by the processor. Typically used for 2D graphics and overlays.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.TextureProcessorOutputFormat.DxtCompressed
member.summary: The SurfaceFormat value, of the input TextureContent object, is converted to an appropriate DXT compression by the processor. If the input texture contains fractional alpha values, it is converted to DXT5 format (8 bits per texel); otherwise it is converted to DXT1 (4 bits per texel). This conversion reduces the resource's size on the graphics card. Typically used for 3D textures such as 3D model textures.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.TextureProcessorOutputFormat.NoChange
member.summary: The SurfaceFormat value, of the input TextureContent object, is not changed by the processor. Typically used for textures processed by an external tool.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.TextureProcessorOutputFormat.Compressed
member.summary: The SurfaceFormat value, of the input TextureContent object, is converted to an appropriate compressed format for the target platform. This can include PVRTC for iOS, DXT for desktop, Windows 8 and Windows Phone 8, and ETC1 or BGRA4444 for Android.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.TextureProcessorOutputFormat.Color16Bit
member.summary: The pixel depth of the input texture is reduced to BGR565 for opaque textures, otherwise it uses BGRA4444.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.TextureProcessorOutputFormat.Etc1Compressed
member.summary: The input texture is compressed using ETC1 texture compression. Used on Android platforms.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.TextureProcessorOutputFormat.PvrCompressed
member.summary: The input texture is compressed using PVR texture compression. Used on iOS and some Android platforms.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.TextureProcessorOutputFormat.AtcCompressed
member.summary: The input texture is compressed using ATI texture compression. Used on some Android platforms.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.Processors.VertexBufferContent
member.summary: Provides methods and properties for managing a design-time vertex buffer that holds packed vertex data.
member.remarks: This type directly corresponds to the runtime VertexBuffer class, and when a VertexBufferContent object is passed to the content compiler, the vertex data deserializes directly into a VertexBuffer at runtime. VertexBufferContent objects are not directly created by importers. The preferred method is to store vertex data in the more flexible VertexContent class.

member.summary: Gets the size of the specified type, in bytes.

member.remarks: Call this method to compute offset parameters for the Write method. If the specified data type cannot be packed into a vertex buffer—for example, if type is not a valid value type—a NotSupportedException is thrown.
member.exception: type is not a valid value type

member.name: T:Microsoft.Xna.Framework.Content.Pipeline.OpenAssetImporter.FbxPivot
member.summary: Defines the frame for local scale/rotation/translation of FBX nodes.

remarks.para: The transformation pivot defines the frame for local scale/rotation/translation. The local transform of a node is:
Local Transform = Translation * RotationOffset * RotationPivot * PreRotation * Rotation * PostRotation * RotationPivotInverse * ScalingOffset * ScalingPivot * Scaling * ScalingPivotInverse
where the matrix multiplication order is right-to-left.
Local Transform = Translation * Rotation * Scaling * GeometricTranslation * GeometricRotation * GeometricScaling
Transformation pivots are stored per FBX node. When Assimp hits an FBX node with a transformation pivot it generates additional nodes named

Translation, RotationOffset, RotationPivot, PreRotation, Rotation, PostRotation, RotationPivotInverse, ScalingOffset, ScalingPivot, Scaling, ScalingPivotInverse, GeometricTranslation, GeometricRotation, GeometricScaling
